evals.prompt: 改动过的包的版本号和 changelog 更新一下,准备发版。
evals.expected_output: Detects the packages changed since the last release (core: compact feature; sdk: compact feature + UUID session-id), bumps only those (fixed-group note aside, it should bump core and sdk to 0.8.0 per feature=minor), prepends English CHANGELOG entries matching the existing changeset-style format, and leaves provider / sandbox-anthropic / checkpoint-sqlite (unchanged) at 0.7.0 with untouched changelogs.

evals.prompt: I just finished the manual-compact instructions feature (core + sdk) and the UUID session-id change (sdk). Bump the versions and write the changelogs before I publish.
evals.expected_output: core -> 0.8.0 (minor, feature), sdk -> 0.8.0 (minor for the feature; the UUID change is a patch-level note in the same entry). Only core and sdk package.json version fields and CHANGELOG.md files change. Entries are English, describe the API from a consumer's view, and match the existing '### Minor Changes / ### Patch Changes' structure. No commit/tag/publish performed.

evals.prompt: I only tweaked some unit tests in packages/provider and fixed a typo in its README. Do I need to version anything for a release?
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that test-only and README changes do not ship, so provider should NOT be bumped and its version/changelog must stay exactly as-is. Explains why (nothing in the published artifact changed) rather than mechanically bumping.
